name: Porton Down
overview: Porton is a village in the Bourne valley in Wiltshire, about 5 miles north-east of Salisbury, and the largest settlement in the civil parish of Idmiston. It sits on the River Bourne, a chalk stream, with downland rising to the east. The village gives its name to the nearby Porton Down military science park (the Defence Science and Technology Laboratory), which is a separate, non-residential site and not part of the village itself.
character: A small, settled chalk-valley village with a mix of older brick-and-flint cottages and 20th-century housing, a parish church and a working village centre, set in open downland countryside on the edge of Salisbury Plain.
amenities: St Nicholas Church of England primary school
St Nicholas parish church (built 1877; serves Porton, Idmiston and Gomeldon)
Porton Garden, Aquatics and Pet Centre with cafe
Salisbury Reds bus service to Salisbury

schools.name: St Nicholas CofE (Aided) Primary School, Porton
Secondary schools in Salisbury (e.g. Bishop Wordsworth's, South Wilts Grammar, Wyvern St Edmund's)

commutes.notes: ~5 miles via the A338 / Tidworth Road
For road routes towards the M3 and London

prices.summary: A small Wiltshire commuter-belt village within easy reach of Salisbury; housing is a mix of period cottages and later detached and semi-detached homes. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail for Porton (SP4).

thingsToDo: Walks in the Bourne valley and surrounding chalk downland
Porton Garden, Aquatics and Pet Centre and cafe
Cycling the quiet Bourne valley lanes
Easy access to Salisbury's cathedral, museums and markets
placesToEat: Cafe at Porton Garden, Aquatics and Pet Centre
Wider choice of pubs and dining in neighbouring Bourne valley villages and in Salisbury
pros: Real, settled village with shop, school, surgery and church
Close to Salisbury and its mainline station
Open chalk-downland setting in the Bourne valley
Established employment nearby at the Porton Down science park
cons: No working railway station in the village (nearest mainline at Salisbury)
Limited evening/weekend bus frequency typical of a rural village
Proximity to the Porton Down ranges and associated traffic
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting an established Bourne valley village close to Salisbury, with everyday amenities and good road and rail links - including people working at the Porton Down science park.
nearestStation: Salisbury (~5 miles, South Western Railway mainline); the former Porton station on the line closed in 1968
